hook.text: Danish researcher Bent Flyvbjerg spent decades collecting data on mega-project cost overruns.

buildup.text: He analyzed thousands of projects: bridges, tunnels, dams, IT systems, power plants, Olympic Games.

discovery.text: His finding: 9 out of 10 mega-projects exceed their budgets. The average overrun is massive and persistent.

twist.text: Flyvbjerg argues that overruns aren't mistakes — they're features of how projects get approved.

climax.text: His work made 'reference class forecasting' a standard tool in fighting the planning fallacy.

punchline.text: One researcher proved the entire world underestimates projects.

quiz.question: What did Flyvbjerg find about mega-project budgets?
options: 9 out of 10 exceed their budgets with massive overruns
Only IT projects have cost overruns
